title: Commission takes preparatory steps on financial support for Ukraine and boosting drone production
content: On 1st April 2026, the European Commission took preparatory steps for the implementation of the €90 billion Ukraine Support Loan, aimed at securing necessary budgetary support and accelerating urgent defence procurement for Ukraine in 2026 and 2027.

title: Trends in PES: Insights from the 2025 Capacity Report
content: The European Network of Public Employment Services (PES Network) has just published its latest ‘Assessment report on PES capacity.’ The report provides an overview of the services Public Employment Services offer, latest trends and challenges they are facing.

title: EU guidance targets energy transition in aquaculture sector
content: The European Commission has released a new staff working document outlining how the aquaculture sector can reduce its carbon footprint through improved energy efficiency and increased use of renewable energy.

title: Following WTO ruling favourable to EU, China announces withdrawal of its anti-suit injunction policy
content: China has announced the withdrawal of its anti-suit injunction policy after it was found to violate WTO rules.
snippet: China has announced the withdrawal of its anti-suit injunction policy after it was found to violate WTO rules.

title: Youth advocates take centre stage in Housing Dialogue
content: On 27 March, European Commissioner for Energy and Housing Dan Jørgensen met with youth representatives in Brussels for a Housing Youth Policy Dialogue.
snippet: On 27 March, European Commissioner for Energy and Housing Dan Jørgensen met with youth representatives in Brussels for a Housing Youth Policy Dialogue.

title: Wildfire preparedness in Europe: Turning innovation into operational capability
content: On 12 March, a CERIS workshop explored how innovative European technologies can strengthen wildfire prevention, preparedness and response – and how innovation procurement can bring them into operation.
snippet: On 12 March, a CERIS workshop explored how innovative European technologies can strengthen wildfire prevention, preparedness and response – and how innovation procurement can bring them into operation

title: EU and Australia formally open negotiations on the association to Horizon Europe
content: Research and innovation news alert: Following the visit of President von der Leyen to Australia, the European Commission and Australia have today opened formal negotiations for the association to Horizon Europe, the EU’s flagship research and innovation programme.

title: Commission issues guidance for pragmatic and proportionate rules on protecting wild birds
content: The Commission document will help Member States protect bird populations while reducing unnecessary administrative burden.
snippet: The Commission document will help Member States protect bird populations while reducing unnecessary administrative burden.

title: 2025 most destructive EU wildfire season on record
content: Fires started early in the year and intensified during summer heatwaves.
snippet: Fires started early in the year and intensified during summer heatwaves.

title: Tax revenue rebounds in 2024 after decade-low in 2023
content: The Taxation Trends dataset provides a detailed overview of key taxation indicators, enabling analysis of tax systems from multiple perspectives—by tax type, economic function, government level, and implicit tax rates.

title: The Entry/Exit System will become fully operational on 10 April 2026
content: The new Entry/Exit System (EES) will replace passport stamping with digitally recorded entries, exits or refusals of entry of non-EU nationals coming for short stays. Travellers’ facial image, fingerprints and personal data from the travel document will be also recorded.

title: New EU initiative bolsters investment in circular water systems
content: The newly established initiative will support public and private entities in developing and implementing circular economy investments in the water sector.
snippet: The newly established initiative will support public and private entities in developing and implementing circular economy investments in the water sector.

title: Commission approves €6 billion Italian State aid scheme for renewable hydrogen
content: Commission approves €6 billion Italian State aid scheme for renewable hydrogen
snippet: Commission approves €6 billion Italian State aid scheme for renewable hydrogen

title: Commission approves €6 billion Italian State aid scheme for renewable hydrogen
content: The European Commission has approved, under EU State aid rules, a €6 billion Italian scheme to support the production of renewable hydrogen for the transport and industrial sectors.
snippet: The European Commission has approved, under EU State aid rules, a €6 billion Italian scheme to support the production of renewable hydrogen for the transport and industrial sectors.

title: Commission report shows progress in boosting Europe’s social economy
content: The Commission has published a report of its Action Plan for the Social Economy, reviewing its progress since 2021 and setting out further actions to strengthen the social economy across Europe.
snippet: The Commission has published a report of its Action Plan for the Social Economy, reviewing its progress since 2021 and setting out further actions to strengthen the social economy across Europe.
